en.extract:
The Sri Lanka thrush or Sri Lanka scaly thrush (Zoothera imbricata) is a member of the thrush family Turdidae. This bird is an endemic resident breeder found in south western rainforests of the island of Sri Lanka.
Zoothera imbricata was formerly treated as a race of the scaly thrush. It belongs in a group, possibly a superspecies, formed by that species and Z. lunulata, Z. heinei, Z. machiki, Z. talaseae, Z. margaretae etc., Z. imbricata being smaller, longer billed and rufous below.


References


External links
In Internet Bird Collection

The '''Sri Lanka thrush''' or '''Sri Lanka scaly thrush''' ('''''Zoothera imbricata''''') is a member of the [[Thrush (bird)|thrush]] family [[Thrush (bird)|Turdidae]]. This bird is an [[endemic]] resident breeder found in south western [[rainforests]] of the island of [[Sri Lanka]].

''Zoothera imbricata'' was formerly treated as a race of the [[scaly thrush]]. It belongs in a group, possibly a [[superspecies]], formed by that species and ''Z. lunulata'', ''Z. heinei'', ''Z. machiki'', ''Z. talaseae'', ''Z. margaretae'' etc.,<ref>{{cite web |url=http://ibc.lynxeds.com/species/sri-lanka-scaly-thrush-zoothera-imbricata |title=Sri Lanka Scaly Thrush (Zoothera imbricata) {{!}} the Internet Bird Collection |website=ibc.lynxeds.com |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090606202549/http://ibc.lynxeds.com/species/sri-lanka-scaly-thrush-zoothera-imbricata |archive-date=2009-06-06}} </ref> ''Z. imbricata'' being smaller, longer [[beak|billed]] and rufous below.
